notes: Phase 7 reconciles markdown, JSON, CLI help snapshots, and current-state truth for the full 124-flag public surface.
Phase 6 publishes the public lifecycle and EmbeddedServer contract with ordering and failure semantics.
Phase 5 closes --ssl-keyfile-password and --ssl-crl as implemented TLS material-input surfaces.
Phase 4 closes the dedicated HTTP/2 operator surface with max-streams, header/frame sizing, adaptive/initial window, and keepalive ping controls.
Phase 3 closes HTTP/1.1 parser/request-head and WebSocket queue controls with CLI/config/env/docs/test parity.
Phase 9F3 closes --limit-concurrency, --websocket-ping-interval, and --websocket-ping-timeout as implemented scheduler/keepalive runtime surfaces.
Phase 9F2 closes --log-config, --statsd-host, and --otel-endpoint as implemented observability surfaces.
Phase 9F1 closes --ssl-ciphers as an implemented TLS/QUIC cipher-policy surface.
All current public flag rows are now promotion-ready; remaining promotion blockers live in strict-target RFC evidence and the strict performance target.

Phase 1 surface parity checkpoint adds contracts for env-file, runtime selection, worker healthcheck timeout, default/date headers, server-name allowlists, unix socket ownership controls, and colorized logging toggles.
Phase 4 advanced delivery adds concrete public flag contracts for Alt-Svc advertisement and auto-advertisement controls.
Step 2 descopes trio from the public runtime surface until an end-to-end trio server core is implemented.
Phase 2 adds server-native static route/mount/index/expires controls and closes standard ASGI http.response.pathsend-backed file delivery.

contracts.claim_text: Hybrid contract for --http1-buffer-size at http.http1_buffer_size. This HTTP/1.1-specific buffering control is not RFC-defined, but it must preserve the declared request-body streaming and request-head parsing surfaces under supported deployment profiles.

status.release_note: Phase 3 adds an HTTP/1.1 buffer-size control for request-head/body incremental reads.

contracts.claim_text: Hybrid contract for --http1-header-read-timeout at http.http1_header_read_timeout. This HTTP/1.1-specific phase timeout tightens the generic read/keep-alive timeout during request-head parsing while preserving RFC 9112 request semantics.

status.release_note: Phase 3 adds a dedicated HTTP/1.1 request-head read-timeout control.

contracts.claim_text: Hybrid contract for --http1-keep-alive at http.http1_keep_alive. This HTTP/1.1-specific persistence toggle is not RFC-defined, but it must preserve request/response correctness while explicitly controlling connection reuse.

status.release_note: Phase 3 adds an explicit HTTP/1.1 keep-alive enable/disable contract.

contracts.claim_text: Hybrid contract for --http1-max-incomplete-event-size at http.http1_max_incomplete_event_size. This HTTP/1.1-specific parser budget is not itself RFC-defined, but it must preserve RFC 9112 request-head parsing semantics under supported load and deployment profiles.

status.release_note: Phase 3 adds an HTTP/1.1 request-head incomplete-event cap with CLI/config/env/docs parity.

contracts.claim_text: Hybrid contract for --http2-adaptive-window at http.http2_adaptive_window. This HTTP/2 flow-control tuning surface is not itself RFC-defined, but it must preserve RFC 9113 receive-window legality while allowing the package-owned adaptive replenishment policy to grow connection and stream windows under observed load.

status.release_note: Phase 4 adds an adaptive HTTP/2 receive-window policy that can grow connection and stream targets while preserving flow-control legality.

contracts.claim_text: Hybrid contract for --http2-initial-connection-window-size at http.http2_initial_connection_window_size. This HTTP/2 flow-control tuning surface is not itself RFC-defined, but it must preserve RFC 9113 legality while increasing the connection receive window via stream-0 WINDOW_UPDATE when configured above the default.

status.release_note: Phase 4 adds explicit HTTP/2 connection receive-window sizing and emits the matching stream-0 WINDOW_UPDATE increment.

contracts.claim_text: Hybrid contract for --http2-initial-stream-window-size at http.http2_initial_stream_window_size. This HTTP/2 flow-control tuning surface is not itself RFC-defined, but it must preserve RFC 9113 legality while advertising a bounded SETTINGS_INITIAL_WINDOW_SIZE value for newly opened streams.

status.release_note: Phase 4 adds explicit HTTP/2 initial stream receive-window sizing and advertises it in local SETTINGS.

contracts.claim_text: Hybrid contract for --http2-keep-alive-interval at http.http2_keep_alive_interval. This keepalive scheduling surface is not itself RFC-defined, but it must preserve RFC 9113 correctness while scheduling connection-level PING probes on idle HTTP/2 transports.

status.release_note: Phase 4 adds HTTP/2 connection keepalive scheduling through periodic PING frames.

contracts.claim_text: Hybrid contract for --http2-keep-alive-timeout at http.http2_keep_alive_timeout. This keepalive timeout surface is not itself RFC-defined, but it must preserve RFC 9113 correctness while closing stale HTTP/2 transports when configured PING probes are not acknowledged in time.

status.release_note: Phase 4 adds HTTP/2 keepalive timeout enforcement for unacknowledged connection PING probes.

contracts.claim_text: Hybrid contract for --http2-max-concurrent-streams at http.http2_max_concurrent_streams. This HTTP/2-specific concurrency control is not itself RFC-defined, but it must preserve declared RFC 9113 behavior while advertising an explicit SETTINGS_MAX_CONCURRENT_STREAMS value.

status.release_note: Phase 4 adds a dedicated HTTP/2 max-concurrent-streams control and advertises it in local SETTINGS.

contracts.claim_text: Hybrid contract for --http2-max-frame-size at http.http2_max_frame_size. This HTTP/2-specific frame-size control is not itself RFC-defined, but it must preserve RFC 9113 framing correctness while advertising a bounded SETTINGS_MAX_FRAME_SIZE value.

status.release_note: Phase 4 adds a dedicated HTTP/2 max-frame-size control and advertises it in local SETTINGS.

contracts.claim_text: Hybrid contract for --http2-max-headers-size at http.http2_max_headers_size. This HTTP/2-specific header budgeting control is not itself RFC-defined, but it must preserve HPACK decoding and request acceptance while advertising a bounded SETTINGS_MAX_HEADER_LIST_SIZE value.

status.release_note: Phase 4 adds a dedicated HTTP/2 max-headers-size control and advertises it in local SETTINGS.

exit_tests: flag_contracts.json marks --limit-concurrency as implemented and promotion_ready true
live admission caps reject or defer work consistently at the configured limit
correctness-under-load artifacts preserve overload behavior without RFC regressions

validation_rules: Accepts colon-separated or comma-separated TLS 1.3 suite names from the supported package-owned allowlist.
Resolves the configured allowlist into config.tls.resolved_cipher_suites and listener.resolved_cipher_suites during normalization.
Invalid expressions fail configuration validation before listener start.
Configured allowlists measurably change the negotiated suite on both TCP/TLS and QUIC/TLS paths.
contracts.claim_text: RFC-scoped contract for --ssl-ciphers at tls.ciphers. The flag now survives config normalization into resolved TLS 1.3 allowlists and changes the negotiated package-owned TCP/TLS and QUIC/TLS cipher suite set when configured.

status.release_note: Phase 9F1 closes --ssl-ciphers by wiring package-owned TLS and QUIC cipher-suite selection to the resolved public allowlist.

artifact_contract.required_claim: Configured cipher policy changes the negotiated package-owned TLS/QUIC suite set and invalid values fail fast.

exit_tests: flag_contracts.json marks --ssl-ciphers as implemented and promotion_ready true
configured allowlists measurably change negotiated suites on TCP/TLS and QUIC/TLS paths
invalid expressions fail validation before listener start
